title: Lemon Olive Oil Cake
description: This lemon olive oil cake recipe is incredibly moist and filled with bright, fruity flavor. Made with simple ingredients, it's an easy, delicious treat!

content.text: Okay, seriously, this lemon olive oil cake is my go-to spring dessert now. It's unbelievably moist and tender, with that perfect zing from lemon juice and zest. Plus, the olive oil just makes it *amazing*.

content.title: Baking Tip
content.text: Room temperature eggs are your friend! They help create a smoother, lighter batter – trust me.

Honestly, this recipe is a breeze. Super simple ingredients and you don't need a ton of fancy equipment. It's perfect for when you want something special but don't want to spend all day in the kitchen. Great with coffee or tea!
Here's what you'll grab: olive oil, flour, fresh lemons (the star!), baking powder, baking soda, eggs, sugar, milk, vanilla, and a pinch of salt.

content.title: Ingredient Tip
content.text: Sift that flour and baking powder together! It prevents lumps and keeps the cake nice and light.
How to Make Olive Oil Cake
First things first: the batter. Whisk together all the dry stuff in one bowl. Then, in a bigger bowl, combine the lemon zest and sugar, then add the wet ingredients and whisk it up.
Gently add the dry ingredients to the wet and whisk until *just* combined.

content.title: Mixing Tip
content.text: Don't overmix! Seriously, stop when it's just combined. Overmixing = a dense cake, and nobody wants that.
Pour the batter into a greased cake pan and bake at 350°F for 40-50 minutes.

content.title: Baking Tip
content.text: Give the pan a little tap on the counter before baking to get rid of any air bubbles.

content.title: Quick Tip
content.text: If the top starts to get too brown, tent it with foil. No burnt cake allowed!
How to Store Olive Oil Cake
Keep it in an airtight container at room temperature for up to 4 days, or in the fridge for up to a week.

content.title: Storage Tip
content.text: Let refrigerated cake come to room temperature before serving – it'll taste so much better!

instructions: Preheat oven to 350°F and grease a 9-inch round pan.
Mix lemon zest and sugar, then add wet ingredients.
Pour batter into pan and bake 40–50 minutes.
